author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "It is written, ‘Thou shalt not commit adultery.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Each one of you, even though he be chaste in his body, yet in spirit is an adulterer, because he pollutes his holiness."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: These words also might be spoken with truth against certain both of our number and of yours; but if their deeds are condemned by us and you alike, they belong to neither us nor you.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But you wish that what you say against certain men, without proving it even in their especial case, should be taken just as if you had established it,—not in the case of some who have fallen away from the seed of Abraham, but in reference to all the nations of the earth who are blessed in the seed of Abraham.
